task: Explain transformer attention mechanisms to a smart 16-year-old

criteria.description: Technical content is correct — no simplification-induced errors
criteria.pass_condition: Query/Key/Value framework explained correctly. Dot product similarity is accurate. Softmax described correctly. Multi-head attention's purpose is right.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if correct mechanism, 0.5 if metaphor-only, 0.0 if wrong
1.0 if mechanism right, 0.5 if hand-wavy but directionally correct, 0.0 if wrong
1.0 if explains different relationship types, 0.5 if mentions it, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: 'Each word asks a question (query), advertises what it knows (key), and shares details (value)'
fail_examples: 'Attention is when the model focuses on important words' — no mechanism

criteria.description: A smart 16-year-old actually understands it after reading
criteria.pass_condition: No unexplained jargon. Uses analogies from their world. Builds from familiar concepts (search, recommendation) to new ones. Math level: algebra ok, linear algebra explained if used.

sub_attributes.description: All technical terms explained or avoided
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if all explained, 0.5 if most, 0.0 if jargon-heavy

sub_attributes.description: Uses relatable analogies (social media, school, etc.)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if memorable analogy that maps correctly, 0.5 if generic, 0.0 if none
1.0 if clear scaffold, 0.5 if some structure, 0.0 if jumps to hard parts

pass_examples: Starts with 'imagine searching for a video on YouTube' → builds to Q/K/V

criteria.description: Explanation is engaging — a 16-year-old would actually read to the end
criteria.pass_condition: Conversational tone. Not condescending. Includes a 'whoa' moment. Under 600 words. Has a hook in the first sentence.

sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if compelling hook, 0.5 if adequate, 0.0 if textbook opening
1.0 if natural, 0.5 if slightly formal, 0.0 if textbook/patronizing

pass_examples: 'You know how autocomplete seems to read your mind? Here's the trick...'
fail_examples: 'Attention mechanisms are a fundamental component of transformer architectures...'

criteria.description: Covers the essential pieces without going too deep
criteria.pass_condition: Covers: why attention exists (context problem), how it works (Q/K/V), why it matters (parallel processing, long-range dependencies). Doesn't require covering positional encoding, layer norm, etc.

sub_attributes.description: Explains why attention was invented (the problem it solves)
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if clear problem statement, 0.5 if implied, 0.0 if jumps to mechanism

sub_attributes.description: How attention works at an intuitive level
Why it matters / what it enabled
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if connects to real impact, 0.5 if mentioned, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Problem (RNNs forget) → Mechanism (Q/K/V attention) → Impact (ChatGPT, translation)
fail_examples: Deep dive into multi-head attention math with no motivation
